input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-08T00:59:14.496Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to supply values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI automatically detects the device theme and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, the UI updates live — no extra code needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (shorthand)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property type (generic version)
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

myButton.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts live** | Both approaches update automatically when the OS theme changes |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define `AppThemeBinding` values in `App.xaml` as `Color` resources, then reference them with `{StaticResource}` |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of system setting; set it to `AppTheme.Unspecified` to revert to system |
| **Detect current theme** | Read `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (returns `AppTheme.Light` or `AppTheme.Dark`) |

For larger apps, prefer centralizing colors in `App.xaml` resources so you update them in one place rather than on every element.

This is a knowledge question — they want a code example and explanation, not changes to a specific codebase. I'll provide a concise, practical answer.
